[26] Fungal Fields // Ammon’s corruption made into a beautific field of fleshy stalks. Stalks of bioluminescent fungus and flesh, 6-8 feet high, fill the cavernous space. ▶Each time a PC enters a fungal field they receive a Whisper Card (p. 34). ▶As the DOOMClock progresses, fields of fungi can appear throughout the mall (sprouting from spores). ▶Across the cavern, a perfectly carved archway pulses with red light. ▶A fungal panther uses the fields as its hunting grounds. Roll d10 on the Navigate the Fungal Fields table. ▶Reroll repeated results. M. FUNGAL FIELDS d10 NAVIGATING THE FUNGAL FIELDS 01 The party gets SEPARATED (p. 23). 02 Stalks of elongated hands reach and grab at you. 03 Every inch of the space is filled with different types of eyes, watching your every move (Stress). 04 One of the stalks seems to have a face. Whoever examines closely finds it to be the face of the person they love (50%) or hate (50%) the most - Whisper Card (p. 37). 05 The stalks behind you jerk, as though something large is coming your way. 06 Surprise attack by the Fungal Panther (p. 24)! It attacks, then runs back into the field. 07 Encounter a Fruiting Body (p. 13) - the party leader gets a Whisper Card (p. 37). 08 An NPC, clothes tattered, crying. 09Endless stalks slowly waving. 10 You make it out the other side, at an opening that leads to the Portal (p. 10). FUNGAL PANTHER STR: 10 DEX: 14 CTRL: 13 HP: 8 Armor: 1 Bite (d8), Tail (d6), Claws (d8+d8) ▶Four multi-jointed limbs ending in razor sharp spurs of bone. Flesh curling back in ribbons. ▶Once was human. ▶The spine has stretched into a boney, prehensile, whip-like appendage extending from the base of its neck. ▶Jagged “fangs” line its multi-hinged mandibles. The Portal // Not everyone needs to be a hero. Sometimes you just need to be the villain. [27] A cavernous space, something dark and primal pulsing within. ▶ If the party came from the Fungal Fields, a small pathway leads up to the ledge, in front of the cavernous room containing the portal. ▶ If the party came from the tunnels (F or K) they arrive on a ledge leading toward the opening. Off to the side is a seemingly endless field of swaying fungalstalks. THE WOMB: This vast expanse is illuminated by the “object” that fills its center. ▶A massive gate of flesh, bone and tumorous fungus. ▶The object bubbles and pulses as it slowly grows and expands. Put the final card face up on the table and read it to the players. THE PORTAL IS OPENING With enough time and organic material, it willtear itself in such a way that it can never be closed. ▶Reality is thin in this space. ▶What is beyond is unclear, but the smell and colors spilling forth are almost overwhelming. ▶With the right toolsand effort, the gate can be destroyed. ▶Players can walk through the portal, but will be unable to return from beyond its arches. N. THE PORTAL As the party approaches the Portal, place this card in the center of the table. ▶ If you have not been REPLACED: You feel an immense sense of dread and wrongness when looking upon this portal. ▶ If you have been REPLACED: Transform and protect the portal. CHILDREN OF AMMON Add d4 to each stat (STR/DEX/CTRL/HP) ▶Damage: d6 - d12 (number of appendages, size, bones, fungus). ▶Metamorphosis: Can change forms in moments, sometimes even breaking off into multiple unique instances. ▶Anointed Blood: Spray a mix of blood, and spores. Witnesses take d4 Stress. ▶Critical Damage: Fleshy tendrils pierce the victim’s flesh, worming their way between muscle and bone (D4 damage to DEX). L E [28] Encounters // While there isn’t an explicit encounter roll mechanic, use these to amp up the tension. ENCOUNTERS Use this tiered encounter table to randomly generate events throughout The Mall. Since the Doom Clock changes in response to player intervention, use it to guide the severity of the encounter. ▶Many of these encounters may trigger a Whisper Card (p. 37). d10TIER 1 (DOOM Clock 3-5) 01 A Mall Rat (p. 7) shouts for help! Their friend fell and got trapped in the vent (near a Fruiting Body p. 13). 02An NPC clutching their stomach complaining about pain. 03A mangled name tag (30% chance it’s readable). 04Someone attempts to break through the barrier. 05Beth the Mall Rat (p. 7), searching for supplies. 06Griffith (p. 7) or Maggie (p. 6) giving a speech. 07Movement in the vents. 08A trail of blood leading to a random shop (p. 5). 09 A band of NPCs surrounding someone, accusing them of being infected. 10A Child of Ammon attacks (p. 9). d10TIER 2 (DOOM Clock 6-8) 01 An NPC proclaims, “It dwells below our feet!” right before they jump from the topmost level. 02Someone attempting to "cut it out." 03 A fleshless “Angel” (p. 9) attacks from the shadows, attempting to subdue and consume. 04A fire breaks out in a random shop (p. 5). 05A large cloud of spores drifts through the mall. 06Beth (p. 7) offering a safe space to a blood-covered NPC. 07Open physical conflict between two factions. 08Recent human remains, with large pieces missing. 09 A Child Of Ammon in the form of a rabbit-parakeet-kitten chimera goes on a rampage (p. 9). 10A band of NPCs killing someone accused of being infected. TIER 3 Encounters // DOOM is all but certain. Can the PCs die on their own terms? [29] d4TIER 3 (DOOM Clock 9+) 01 The ground shudders as a sinkhole consumes the roller rink, leading directly to the Fungal Fields (p. 26) below the mall. 02A pack of Fungal Panthers (p. 26) hunt through the mall. 03People on the “hunt.” 04The culling begins. ENCOUNTERSB PERSONNEL 1. Mr. Neil -
